evidence: WAPT skill list contains: `hunt-race-condition hunt-race-condition`, `hunt-cache-poison hunt-cache-poison`, `hunt-subdomain hunt-subdomain` — each appears on two consecutive lines
description: Dispatcher taxonomy WAPT list has three duplicate skill entries causing each to appear twice in operator selection output
suggested_fix: Deduplicate: ensure hunt-race-condition, hunt-cache-poison, and hunt-subdomain each appear exactly once in the WAPT array

evidence: clones https://github.com/shuvonsec/claude-bug-bounty without commit hash pin, then runs `chmod +x install.sh && echo n | ./install.sh`
description: Optional community-skills installer fetches and executes third-party code without commit pinning; drift in upstream repo silently changes installed skill content
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific commit: `git clone --depth=1 --branch <tag> https://github.com/shuvonsec/claude-bug-bounty.git` or use `git clone` followed by `git checkout <sha>`

evidence: skill has no Gate 0 block, no bypass techniques section, no real-world example; peer skills (hunt-ssrf, cloud-iam-deep) average 10+ structured steps
description: hunt-cloud-misconfig has sparse methodology relative to peer skills; no Gate 0 pre-conditions, no bypass table, no examples
suggested_fix: Add Gate 0 block, a bypass-techniques table (signed URL bypass, metadata SSRF, IMDSv1 vs IMDSv2), and at least one disclosed CVE or report reference

evidence: skill body lacks Gate 0, tooling section, severity scoring guidance; substantially shorter than peer skills in the corpus
description: hunt-llm-ai has sparse methodology; prompt injection and training data exfiltration are briefly mentioned but lack attack-path structure
suggested_fix: Add Gate 0 checks, structured attack steps for prompt injection / indirect injection / exfiltration, and a severity scoring table

evidence: both bb-local-toolkit (~1550 lines) and bug-bounty (~1600 lines) contain 7-Question Gate, IDOR methodology, SSRF bypass table, CI/CD deep-dive
description: Significant content overlap between bb-local-toolkit and bug-bounty creates divergence risk; fixes to one do not propagate to the other
suggested_fix: Designate one as canonical and have the other reference it via `Related Skills` section rather than duplicating content
